message:
feat: Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection for agent state transitions

Adds bocpd.rs — a statistical anomaly detection engine that complements
the regex-based pattern engine by detecting regime changes that fixed
patterns cannot catch: infinite output loops, quality degradation,
novel failure modes, and subtle behavioral drift.

Algorithm: Adams & MacKay (2007) BOCPD with Normal-Gamma conjugate
prior. Maintains a run length posterior P(rₜ|x₁:ₜ) updated online
per observation. Each update runs in O(max_run_length) time with a
single-observation target of < 50μs.

Key types:
  - BocpdConfig: hazard_rate, detection_threshold, min_observations,
    max_run_length (all with serde defaults)
  - NormalGammaSS: sufficient statistics for the conjugate prior
    (mu0, kappa, alpha, beta) with predictive_log_likelihood()
  - RunLengthPosterior: the core detector — tracks growth/change
    probabilities, run length distribution, and max-posterior
  - ChangePoint: detected regime change with timestamp, posterior
    probability, old/new mean+variance, and run length
  - BocpdTracker: per-pane wrapper managing observations, feature
    extraction, and change-point history
  - BocpdEngine: multi-pane orchestrator with HashMap<pane_id, Tracker>

Feature extraction from terminal output:
  - Output rate (lines/sec)
  - Mean line length
  - Entropy (character-level Shannon entropy)

Includes 22 unit tests covering:
  - Constant stream → no false positives
  - Mean shift detection (jump from 10.0 to 50.0)
  - Variance change detection
  - Gradual drift detection
  - Min observation warm-up period
  - Run length truncation at max_run_length
  - Config defaults validation
  - Feature extraction (rate, length, entropy)
  - Multi-pane engine orchestration
  - Empty/single observation edge cases
  - Posterior normalization invariant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
